column2: A 32-bit operating system is commonly limited to approximately {{c1::4 GB}} of addressable memory.
column3: A 32-bit address space contains 2^32 possible values. Application and OS architecture must still be checked separately from the amount of installed RAM.
column4: This fact explains a common 32-bit system limitation; it does not mean every 64-bit application will run merely because a computer has more than 4 GB of RAM.
column8: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.10; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 22
